title: rubus briareus
intro: Rubus briareus, also known as the thimbleberry, is a deciduous shrub native to North America, characterized by its edible fruit and medicinal properties. The plant is rich in antioxidants, flavonoids, and phenolic acids, which contribute to its medicinal properties. Thimbleberry is used to treat various health issues, including digestive problems, inflammation, and skin conditions, due to its anti-inflammatory and antibacterial properties. The herbal preparations of Rubus briareus include teas, infusions, and tinctures, which can be consumed to reap its health benefits, such as promoting gut health and reducing oxidative stress.
